meta.title: About — Astro 7 Starter Theme
meta.description: Astro Rocket is a production-ready Astro 7 starter theme with 12 themes, 57+ components, built-in i18n, dark mode, and everything you need to launch fast.

hero.description: A free, production-ready Astro 7 starter theme with everything you need to build fast, modern websites — without starting from scratch.

intro.heading: A solid foundation for any project
paragraphs: Astro Rocket is a production-ready starter theme built on Astro 7. It ships with a full component library, 12 hand-crafted themes, dark mode, built-in i18n, and a blog — so you can focus on building your project, not the boilerplate.
It's free, MIT licensed, and listed on the official Astro themes directory. Clone it, customise it, and ship something you're proud of.

facts.title: Astro 7
facts.description: Built on the latest Astro release.

facts.title: Dark mode
facts.description: Built in, no extra setup needed.

facts.title: Built-in i18n
facts.description: Multi-language support out of the box.

principles.heading: Built with intention
principles.lead: Every decision in Astro Rocket — from component structure to default config — is driven by the same three principles.

items.title: Performance First
items.description: Zero JavaScript by default. Every component is optimised for speed, Core Web Vitals, and lean output.

items.title: Developer Experience
items.description: TypeScript throughout, sensible defaults, and a component library you can drop straight into any page.

items.title: Modern from the Ground Up
items.description: Astro 7, Tailwind CSS 4, and a clean architecture that's easy to extend and maintain.

stack.title: The Stack
stack.description: The technologies powering Astro Rocket — chosen for performance, great developer experience, and long-term reliability.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Everything you need to know before getting started.

items.question: What is Astro?
items.answer: Astro is a modern web framework built for content-driven sites. It ships zero JavaScript by default — only sending what the page actually needs. The result is fast load times, excellent Core Web Vitals scores, and lean, maintainable code.

items.question: Why build with Astro?
items.answer: Astro is the right tool for content-heavy sites: minimal JavaScript, clean HTML, great SEO out of the box, and a developer experience that stays out of your way. It's lean, purposeful, and built to last.

items.question: What can I build with Astro Rocket?
items.answer: Portfolios, business sites, marketing pages, blogs, documentation sites — any content-driven project. Astro Rocket gives you the structure, components, and defaults to hit the ground running.

items.question: Is Astro Rocket listed on the official Astro themes directory?
items.answer: Yes. Astro Rocket is listed on the official Astro themes directory as a free, production-ready theme.

items.question: Will Astro Rocket keep up with new Astro releases?
items.answer: Yes. Astro Rocket is actively maintained and updated to track new Astro releases. Follow along on the blog or watch the GitHub repository for updates.

items.question: Is it free to use?
items.answer: Yes, completely free. Astro Rocket is MIT licensed — use it for personal and commercial projects, no attribution required.

items.question: How do I switch themes?
items.answer: Change a single line in your config. Astro Rocket ships with 12 hand-crafted colour themes — swap between them instantly without touching a line of CSS.

items.question: Does it support TypeScript?
items.answer: Yes, TypeScript is used throughout. All components, configs, and utilities are fully typed.

items.question: How do I add my own language for i18n?
items.answer: Astro Rocket's i18n is config-driven. Add your locale to the i18n config file, create a matching translation file, and the routing and UI adapt automatically.

items.question: Where do I report bugs or suggest features?
items.answer: Open an issue or start a discussion on the GitHub repository. Contributions and feedback are always welcome.

openSource.heading: Built in the open
openSource.description: Astro Rocket is free, open source, and MIT licensed. Browse the source, open issues, submit PRs, or just clone it and build something great.

cards.title: Made by Hans Martens
cards.text: Astro Rocket was designed and built by Hans Martens — a web designer and developer who uses it as the foundation for client projects.

cards.title: MIT License
cards.text: Free to use in personal and commercial projects. No attribution required, though a star on GitHub is always appreciated.

cards.title: Actively maintained
cards.text: The theme is kept up to date with new Astro releases and regularly improved based on community feedback.

cta.heading: Ready to build?
cta.description: Clone Astro Rocket, pick a theme, and launch your next project in minutes.

meta.title: Services — Web Design, Development & Performance
meta.description: Three core services for modern websites: thoughtful design, clean Astro development, and measurable performance & SEO.

hero.description: Three core services, one consistent standard. From the first wireframe to the final Lighthouse score, every project gets the same attention to detail.

design.heading: Design with intention
paragraphs: Great design starts with the people who'll use it. I design layouts shaped around your content and your audience, not around trends that age badly. Typography, spacing, and colour are chosen for clarity — not novelty.

Every screen is considered: desktop, tablet, and the phone someone holds at the bus stop. The brand stays consistent across the whole experience, and the small details are treated as part of the design, not the polish.

included.text: built for your content, not from a template. Every section earns its place by serving the page's goal.
phone, tablet, desktop, ultrawide. Layouts reflow with intent, not just shrink to fit.
readable, scaleable, on-brand. A clear hierarchy from page title down to caption, tuned for long-form reading.
one design, two moods, user's choice. Both modes are checked at every step, not retrofitted at the end.
interactions that guide, never distract. Reduced-motion preferences are respected without exception.
contrast, focus, semantic structure. Keyboard paths and screen-reader output are verified before launch.

development.heading: Code that earns its place
paragraphs: I build with Astro because it ships what's needed and nothing else. The result is sites that score well in audits, feel fast in the hand, and don't grow brittle over time.
Architecture matters as much as output: typed content, predictable component boundaries, and a build pipeline a future developer (or future you) can pick up without a guided tour.

included.text: modern stack, minimal JavaScript, clean HTML. The browser renders the page almost instantly, with no hydration tax.
structured blog, projects, and data. Schema validation runs at build time, so broken content never ships.
protected, GDPR-aware, hooked into your tools. Submissions reach your inbox or CRM without leaking client-side.
Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare, or your own infra. Preview deploys for every branch, so changes can be reviewed before they go live.
git-based workflow, repeatable deploys. Automated checks gate every pull request before merge.
code, keys, and a README you can actually read. Written so a future developer (or future you) can pick the project up without a tour.

performance.heading: Fast pages, found pages
paragraphs: Speed and search visibility aren't features to add at the end — they're constraints to honour from the first commit. Every second a page loads costs attention, ranking, and trust.
I work to measurable targets, not vibes. Core Web Vitals, technical SEO, and accessibility are tracked, tuned, and verified before launch — and the dashboards stay yours after handover.

included.text: LCP, INP, and CLS held to Google's thresholds. Measured on real devices, not just synthetic tests.
modern formats, responsive sizes, lazy loading. Hero images art-directed so they look right on every screen.
metadata, sitemaps, structured data, OG images. All wired up and validated before the first crawl.
WCAG-aware markup and verified focus order. Tested with a screen reader and the keyboard alone, not just a contrast checker.
measurable scores delivered with the site. Repeatable in CI so they don't quietly drift after launch.
useful data, no cookie banner. Self-hosted or vendor-anonymised, never sold on.

process.heading: A clear, honest process
process.lead: Four stages, one point of contact, no surprises.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.description: A conversation about your goals, your audience, and what success looks like. No obligation.

steps.title: Proposal
steps.description: A clear scope, timeline, and cost. One page, plain language, fixed price.

steps.title: Design & Build
steps.description: Short cycles with regular check-ins. You see the site evolve as it's built.

steps.title: Launch & Support
steps.description: Deploy, hand over, stay available. Iteration after launch is straightforward.
